title: F043 — Divided Heart

requiredObjects: central symbolic heart or integration point

composition.layout: central subject with supporting explanatory structure
visualHierarchy: central symbolic heart or integration point
composition.colorLogic: coherent symbolic palette with print-safe contrast

qaChecklist: Avoids forbidden objects and fake text
Does not overuse mystical or decorative elements

prompts.providerAgnostic:
Create a publication-quality scientific book figure titled "F043 — Divided Heart".
Purpose: The divided heart is mapped as fear, shame, resentment, longing, grace, and healing seeking integration.

Manuscript context: Emotional Sobriety and Recursive Healing Purpose This chapter connects FHQCM to recovery, trauma integration, moral inventory, the divided heart, Red Road practice, AA language, and the movement from fragmentation toward harmonic coherence. It treats emotional sobriety as a lived laboratory for the model. If FHQCM claims that reality moves from fragmentation toward coherence through observation, relation, surrender, and repair, then recovery provides an embodied place to test that language. F043 — Divided HeartThe divided heart is mapped as fear, shame, resentment, longing, grace, and healing seeking integration.F043-divided-heart The Divided Heart Fragmentation is not merely an abstract metaphysical concept. It is experienced in the body as fear, resentment, shame, craving, dissociation, compulsion, and reactivity. A person can be physically sober while still emotionally intoxicated by fear, control, martyrdom, rage, fantasy, or despair. In FHQCM language, the divided heart is decoherence at the level of lived personhood. Attention scatters. Memory loops. The body prepares for danger even when no immediate danger is present. The future is interpreted through old wounds. Emotional sobriety begins when the person can observe these patterns without becoming identical to them. Inventory as Observation Moral inventory is a practical form of recursive observation. The person does not merely feel resentment. The person observes resentment. The person asks what fear, wound, expectation, attachment, or false story is embedded inside it.

Scientific/conceptual concepts: observer participation, symbolic integration.
Required visual objects: observer axis or observation node, information flow arrows, central symbolic heart or integration point, spiral or healing path.
Recommended short labels: observer, information, integration, recovery.

Composition: central subject with supporting explanatory structure.
Camera/view: orthographic or clean illustrative perspective.
Visual hierarchy: observer axis or observation node → information flow arrows → central symbolic heart or integration point → spiral or healing path.
Color logic: coherent symbolic palette with print-safe contrast.
Label policy: minimal labels only when useful.

Avoid: fake citations, fake equations, unreadable text, watermarks, copyrighted characters, random mystical clutter, misleading scientific labels, random galaxies unless explicitly required, decorative clutter.

Make it clean, serious, elegant, uncluttered, and suitable for an academic manuscript.
